description_long: This is a large, modular desk system with four substantial rectangular workstations radiating from a central hub, forming a cross-like configuration. Each workstation features a deep, boxy base that likely provides storage or support, and the central area appears to house a set of controls or equipment, possibly for collaborative or technical work. The surfaces are flat and expansive, suitable for multiple users or large projects, and the overall construction is robust and industrial. The desk is predominantly white or light gray, with some darker accents visible in the central section and on a few smaller components. There are additional elements such as a small side table and what appears to be a chair or stool, suggesting a flexible workspace. The design is utilitarian and modern, likely intended for high-traffic or shared environments.
description: A large, cross-shaped modular desk system with four workstations and a central hub, featuring robust boxy bases and a modern, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central hub and two prominent workstations extending left and right, with visible storage bases.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the workstation bases and the central area, with a clear view of the modular structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the central hub and the opposite workstations, with a small side table and chair visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other two workstations and the arrangement of the side table and chair, emphasizing the modularity.
attribute_location: shared office workspace or collaborative laboratory
attribute_orientation: The user-facing side is the one with the central hub and accessible work surfaces, with the front defined by the open area and possible control panel or equipment access.
attribute_usage_context: collaborative workstation for technical, design, or research activities
